dose: 50 mg PO twice daily, 1 week on / 1 week off
baseline_value: 7.9 ± 1.5% (CD4+CD25high of CD4+); 28.7 ± 9.4 cells/mm3 (absolute)
post_value: 3.1 ± 1.8% (CD4+CD25high of CD4+); 6.4 ± 5.4 cells/mm3 (absolute) at 1 month
change_magnitude: ~61% reduction in frequency (7.9% to 3.1%) and ~78% reduction in absolute count (28.7 to 6.4 cells/mm3)
durability_described: Maintained during metronomic dosing; 2/2 patients with extended follow-up had Treg return to pretreatment levels ~2 months after stopping CTX
clinical_correlate: Restored T cell proliferation (17% pre → matching HV post) and NK cytotoxicity (9±1% pre → HV-equivalent post)
notes: Landmark Ghiringhelli 2007 metronomic-cyclo PD paper. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: explicit mean±SD frequency and absolute count values, p<0.0001 for both, functional recovery data, 2-month off-treatment recovery. Side-list Ghiringhelli 2004 preclinical excluded from table.
title: Metronomic cyclophosphamide regimen selectively depletes CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cells and restores T and NK effector functions in end stage cancer patients

dose: Single IV dose (300 mg/m2 per paper)
patient_population: Metastatic cancer patients vs 24 healthy donors
post_value: not significantly changed
change_magnitude: baseline 9.2% (patients) vs 7.1% (healthy) — post-cyclo: no significant modulation
notes: Audia 2007 — single-dose IV cyclo + BCG failed to modulate Treg numbers or function despite elevated baseline in cancer patients. Important null result anchoring the 'dose schedule matters' argument. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: article not openly accessible (not in PMC, Europe PMC inEPMC=N, subscription required). Abstract-derived baseline 9.2% vs 7.1% healthy and 'no significant modulation' remains the only numeric available.
title: Increase of CD4+ CD25+ regulatory T cells in the peripheral blood of patients with metastatic carcinoma: a Phase I clinical trial using cyclophosphamide and immunotherapy to eliminate CD4+ CD25+ T lymphocytes

intervention: Denileukin diftitox (DAB389IL-2) prior to RCC tumor-RNA DC vaccine
combo_partners: Autologous RCC tumor-RNA dendritic cell vaccine
patient_population: Metastatic RCC patients receiving DC vaccine
timepoint_detail: pre- and post-denileukin diftitox; then post-vaccine
baseline_value: 2.5-4.6% CD4+CD25high/FoxP3+ of CD4+ (2.1±1.2-fold above healthy volunteers n=10)
post_value: 26-76% relative reduction of CD4+CD25high Tregs at day 4 post-DAB389IL-2 (all 7 subjects analyzed)
change_magnitude: 26-76% reduction in CD4+CD25high; FoxP3 mRNA reduced ~30-80% within purified CD4+ cells at day 4
durability_described: Transient: ~75% of Tregs restored within 2 months (d4 nadir, d60 recovery)
clinical_correlate: Abrogated in vitro suppressive activity 4 days post-DD; enhanced vaccine-induced tumor antigen T cell responses
notes: Dannull 2005 JCI — first clear demonstration that DAB389IL-2 depletes functional Tregs in cancer patients and enhances vaccine response. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: explicit 26-76% range for frequency reduction, 30-80% for FoxP3 mRNA, nadir d4, 75% recovery by 2 months, suppressive function abrogated.
title: Enhancement of vaccine-mediated antitumor immunity in cancer patients after depletion of regulatory T cells

baseline_value: Not reported in cells/µL; FoxP3 mRNA expression (copies/10^3 β-actin) only
change_magnitude: Small, dose-dependent FoxP3 mRNA reduction; no elimination of CD4+CD25+ cells; suppressive function preserved (50.6-98.2% suppression retained in 5 patients post-cycle-2)
clinical_correlate: 0/13 patients achieved objective response
notes: Attia 2005 — key null result: DAB/IL-2 failed to eliminate functional Tregs in melanoma. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: statistically significant FoxP3 mRNA decrease only in 18 µg/kg cohort (p=0.031), pooled analysis n.s. (p=0.167); in vitro suppression retained in 5/5 patients (50.6-98.2%). Conflicts with Dannull 2005 (16308572): Dannull saw d4 functional abrogation in RCC while Attia saw preserved suppression post-cycle-2 in melanoma; gating (FOXP3+ vs CD4+CD25+) and timepoint differences likely reconcile.
title: Inability of a fusion protein of IL-2 and diphtheria toxin (Denileukin Diftitox, DAB389IL-2, ONTAK) to eliminate regulatory T lymphocytes in patients with melanoma

change_magnitude: significantly reduced; successive doses further reduced
durability_described: Recovery observed over ~13 days after single dose; sustained with repeated dosing
clinical_correlate: Altered immune function in vivo/in vitro
notes: Mahnke 2007 — daclizumab Treg depletion kinetics. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: not openly accessible (Int J Cancer, inEPMC=N, subscription required). Abstract-only values retained: significant PBMC Treg reduction after single bolus, sustained with repeat dosing, ~13-day recovery window.
title: Depletion of CD4+CD25+ human regulatory T cells in vivo: kinetics of Treg depletion and alterations in immune functions in vivo and in vitro

intervention: Daclizumab (anti-CD25) prior to CEA-based cancer vaccine
timepoint_detail: pre- and post- multi-dose daclizumab; then post-vaccine
baseline_value: Baseline CD4+CD25highFoxP3+ frequency (absolute µL count) not tabulated; per-patient trajectories shown in figures
post_value: Cohort 2 (multi-dose, n=4): all 4 patients had lower Treg % at study end; Cohort 1 (single-dose, n=5): only 2/5 reduced
change_magnitude: Dose-schedule dependent: multi-dose DD → 4/4 reduction; single-dose DD → 2/5 reduction; absolute Treg counts per µL began declining at week 5 and continued after final vaccination (Cohort 2 only)
change_significance: Time-to-first immune response p=0.003 (Cohort 2 vs Cohort 0); individual Treg p-values not reported
durability_described: Multi-dose sustained decline through post-vaccine follow-up; single-dose rebounded
clinical_correlate: Enhanced CEA-specific T cell responses in multi-dose cohort (earlier time-to-response, p=0.003)
notes: Morse 2008 Blood — pilot vaccine study. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: schedule-dependent depletion confirmed (4/4 multi-dose vs 2/5 single-dose reduced Treg%); absolute µL counts only in Cohort 2. No tabulated baseline/post numeric means. Conflict pair with Luke 2016 (27330808): both show single-dose DD fails; Morse shows multi-dose DD succeeds — not a true conflict but a schedule-dependence finding.
title: Depletion of human regulatory T cells specifically enhances antigen-specific immune responses to cancer vaccines

change_magnitude: median 56.3% reduction from pre-DD to post-DD
clinical_correlate: Pilot efficacy with HD IL-2 combination
notes: Atchison 2010 pilot. Clean median 56.3% reduction from pre-DD to post-DD, p=0.013 (abstract-reported). Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: article not openly accessible (J Immunother, inEPMC=N, subscription required); abstract numeric retained as sole source.

baseline_value: Not numerically reported; individual patient data shown in Fig 2 without quantification
post_value: Not reproducibly reduced; frequencies similar to control patients
change_magnitude: No reproducible reduction in peripheral Treg numbers; 1/1 evaluable paired tumor biopsy (patient 16) showed INCREASED FoxP3 transcripts post-DD
clinical_correlate: No improvement in vaccine-induced T cell responses vs vaccine alone
notes: Luke 2016 RCT — single-dose DD failed to deplete Tregs or enhance vaccine responses. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: no numeric baseline/post Treg values in text (Fig 2 unquantified); 1 paired tumor biopsy showed INCREASED intratumoral FoxP3 post-DD. Conflict pair with Morse 2008 (18519811): Luke=single-dose→fail; Morse=multi-dose→succeed. Reconciled by schedule dependence, not truly conflicting.

change_magnitude: augmented depletion with IFNα
clinical_correlate: Clinical efficacy augmented by IFNα

post_value: Median relative intra-patient change −29% in CD4+FoxP3+ (94% CI −83% to −20%) after one DD cycle
change_magnitude: Median 29% reduction after one cycle (94% CI −83% to −20%); responders had significant 20-45% absolute number decrease after cycle 1; consecutive cycles produced sustained depletion
durability_described: Transient single-cycle effect with rebound between cycles; sustained with consecutive cycles

notes: Geskin 2018 — CTCL DD study. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: median 29% reduction (94% CI −83%–−20%), p=0.03; responders achieved 20-45% decrease after cycle 1. Skin Treg decrease qualitatively shown in representative patient (unquantified).

post_value: Mean FoxP3 0.0374 ± 0.0101 (n=5) at week 8
change_magnitude: Mean 73% reduction pooled; by dose: 5 µg/kg cohort 58.9% reduction (p=0.1500); 15 µg/kg cohort 83.2% reduction (p=0.0374); 5/9 patients achieved ≥25% reduction (secondary efficacy threshold)
clinical_correlate: 5/9 patients met ≥25% Treg reduction secondary efficacy criterion
notes: Liao 2024 — IP route in ovarian. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: pooled p=0.0275 with 73% reduction; dose-level breakdown shows only 15 µg/kg significant (p=0.0374).
title: Intraperitoneal immunotherapy with denileukin diftitox (ONTAK) in recurrent refractory ovarian cancer

baseline_value: Mean FoxP3 0.1855 ± 0.0945 (n=3) at baseline
post_value: Mean FoxP3 0.0597 ± 0.0304 (n=3) at week 8
change_magnitude: Mean 67% reduction in ascites FoxP3; 3 patients achieved ≥25% reduction (immunologic efficacy threshold)

notes: Liao 2024 ascites compartment. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: 67% mean reduction, n.s. (p=0.2737) — reclassified as 'partial' because direction is consistent with PBMC but underpowered (n=3 paired).
title: Intraperitoneal immunotherapy with denileukin diftitox (ONTAK) in recurrent refractory ovarian cancer

dose: Per protocol, up to six 21-day cycles
baseline_value: Per-patient baseline; aggregate not tabulated
post_value: No significant difference between baseline and maximal observed Treg levels (overall p=0.10)
change_magnitude: Overall depletion n.s. (p=0.10); 6/15 patients (40%) achieved ≥25% Treg reduction with mean reduction 56.0% ± 10.96% (SD) in this responder subset
durability_described: Most reductions occurred after cycle 2; limited by rapid anti-DT IgG response (100% of 9 evaluable patients at week 6, p<0.005)
clinical_correlate: 0/15 CR or PR; 4/15 stable disease; possible anti-DT neutralizing response limited efficacy
notes: Gwin 2025 — partial depletion in breast cancer. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: overall p=0.10 (n.s.); responder subset (6/15, 40%) had 56.0% ± 10.96% mean reduction; 100% anti-DT IgG response by week 6 likely limited efficacy.

notes: Fujikawa 2023 PLoS One — integrated phase 1a/1b mogamulizumab. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: median 2.1% → 0.20% at 4 weeks (~90% reduction); dose-independent (0.1-1.0 mg/kg).

Paper focuses on NK cell expansion and ADCC; no quantitative Treg baseline/post values, p-values, or fractional changes reported. Mechanism of mogamulizumab Treg depletion is assumed by reference to prior moga literature but NOT measured in this trial. title: A phase 1 study of interleukin-15 in combination with mogamulizumab in relapsed and refractory T-cell malignancies

baseline_value: 35.20 ± 30.06 FOXP3+ cells/mm^2 (n=19 paired)
post_value: 167.35 ± 162.37 FOXP3+ cells/mm^2 post-dosing
change_magnitude: Mean +132.15 cells/mm^2 (~4.75× INCREASE) in FOXP3+ density; CR patients 993.76±734.18 vs PD 775.63±458.70 (n.s. p=0.3340)
change_significance: p=0.0029 (paired Mann-Whitney; note: paper uses paired test despite Mann-Whitney label)
clinical_correlate: CD8+ TIL increase independent of clinical response; FOXP3 density trend higher in CR (n.s.)
notes: Huang 2011 — tremelimumab INCREASED rather than depleted intratumoral FOXP3+ cells. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: baseline 35.2 → post 167.4 cells/mm^2 (p=0.0029, paired, n=19). Classic early null-for-intratumoral-Treg-depletion result supporting Sharma 2019's broader conclusion.
title: CTLA4 blockade induces frequent tumor infiltration by activated lymphocytes regardless of clinical responses in humans

baseline_value: Benefit group: FoxP3 detected in 75.0% of evaluable pretreatment biopsies; Non-benefit group: 36.0%
post_value: Longitudinal change not quantified — paper reports baseline FoxP3 only as predictive biomarker
change_magnitude: Not a depletion study; baseline FoxP3 positivity associated with clinical benefit (OR ~10.38, p=0.014); TIL increase post-dose separate finding (p=0.005)
change_significance: p=0.014 (baseline FoxP3 vs clinical benefit); not a post-treatment-change p-value
clinical_correlate: Higher baseline intratumoral FoxP3 → better ipi response (OR 10.38, p=0.014)
notes: Hamid 2011 — predictive biomarker study, NOT a depletion endpoint. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23 confirmed: FoxP3 quantified only at baseline (pretreatment biopsy), not longitudinally; 24-72h post-dose-2 samples collected but FoxP3 change not quantified. Reclassified intent_to_deplete from 'exploratory-but-prespecified' to 'incidental-but-measured' — the study did not aim to measure Treg depletion. Retained in table because it still reports a Treg-related readout in melanoma ipi patients.
title: A prospective phase II trial exploring the association between tumor microenvironment biomarkers and clinical activity of ipilimumab in advanced melanoma

tissue_detail: Stage-matched pre/post samples (melanoma n=19, prostate n=17, bladder n=9); mass cytometry on fresh melanoma n=5
baseline_value: Numeric density values shown only in Fig 2A plots without text quantification
post_value: Numeric density values shown only in Fig 2A plots without text quantification; direction is INCREASE across melanoma (ipi n=16 post vs 19 untreated; treme n=18 paired), no reduction in bladder (n=9) or prostate (n=16)
change_magnitude: Both ipi (Mann-Whitney unpaired) and treme (Wilcoxon paired) significantly INCREASED FOXP3+ density in melanoma, p<0.05; 2 pre-treme patients with zero baseline FOXP3+ gained FOXP3+ infiltrate post-treme; bladder and prostate: no reduction
change_significance: p<0.05 for melanoma cohorts (both ipi and treme); exact p-values not stated in text (figure-only)
notes: Sharma 2019 — foundational human null result. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: both anti-CTLA-4 agents increased rather than depleted intratumoral FOXP3+ cells across melanoma, bladder, and prostate; exact density numbers in Fig 2A plots not textually quantified in paper. Consistent with Huang 2011 (21558401) baseline 35→167 cells/mm^2; both support the absence of Fc-mediated Treg depletion by non-Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 in humans. Conflict pair with Ager 2026 (41759531): Ager shows Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 DOES deplete intratumoral Tregs — the Fc engineering is the reconciling difference.
title: Anti-CTLA-4 Immunotherapy Does Not Deplete FOXP3+ Regulatory T Cells (Tregs) in Human Cancers

baseline_value: Per-patient baseline; aggregate not tabulated
post_value: Median frequency of CD4+CD25+FOXP3+ Tregs increased slightly by 1.05% (V1 chemo-alone to V3 post-ipi)
change_magnitude: Median +1.05% absolute increase in Treg frequency from V1 to V3 (statistically significant but biologically modest); CTLA-4+ Tregs unchanged
notes: Yi 2017 — ipi+chemo neoadj NSCLC. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: median +1.05% Treg frequency increase post-ipi (p=0.012, statistically significant but tiny magnitude and opposite direction from depletion); CTLA-4+ Treg subset unchanged.

post_value: Higher and more variable Treg levels than PBMCs in 7 post-resection tumors; no pre/post comparison

notes: Yi 2017 tumor compartment. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: authors explicitly disclaim ability to assess Treg change in tumor (no pre-treatment biopsy). This row is 'not-assessed' correctly — retained as a documented limitation and because post-treatment tumor Treg data is still in-scope per schema.

intervention: BMS-986218 (Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4) + androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) neoadjuvant
baseline_value: Per-arm baseline TI-Treg frequency; density values not tabulated in text (figure panels only)
post_value: ADT+BMS-986218 arm significantly reduced TI-Treg frequency vs ADT alone (p=0.031); ADT alone arm had INCREASED TI-Tregs vs untreated (p=0.002)
change_magnitude: Qualitative TI-Treg reduction in combo arm; residual TI-Tregs remained in all tumors (incomplete depletion); numeric density values in figure panels without text quantification
change_significance: p=0.031 (ADT+BMS-986218 vs ADT alone); p=0.002 (ADT alone vs untreated, ADT expanded TI-Tregs)

Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: TI-Treg reduction p=0.031 in combo arm; ADT alone expanded TI-Tregs (p=0.002); residual Tregs remained (partial depletion); CD16a+ macrophage correlation p=0.033. Numeric densities in figure panels not text-quantified. Conflict pair with Sharma 2019 (30054281): Sharma shows non-Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 fails to deplete; Ager shows Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 succeeds — reconciled by Fc-engineering difference.
title: Neoadjuvant Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 targets Tregs to augment androgen deprivation in high-risk prostate cancer: A randomized phase I trial

baseline_value: Not quantified in main text; RNA-seq-based signal (Fig 5H)
post_value: Significant intratumoral Treg reduction by IHC and RNA-seq signature (Fig 5H); fold-change and p-value not stated in text

Human PBMC mechanism: not attributed to depletion but to reduced TGFβ1 secretion (Suppl Fig S9B).

clinical_correlate: Monotherapy antitumor activity limited
notes: Vaishampayan 2024 ARTISTRY-1 monotherapy. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: Treg FCBmax ~2x vs NK 6.52x / CD8 2.53x; nemva's IL-2R-βγ bias limited Treg expansion as designed. Kept via ratio-shift path.
title: Nemvaleukin alfa, a modified interleukin-2 cytokine, as monotherapy and with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced solid tumors (ARTISTRY-1)

baseline_value: C1D1 baseline; per-patient data only
post_value: Similar minimal Treg expansion pattern as monotherapy (~2x FCBmax)
change_magnitude: Nemva+pembro: minimal Treg expansion; effector expansion preserved
clinical_correlate: Combination clinical activity
notes: ARTISTRY-1 Part C combo. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: combo cohort showed the same minimal Treg expansion pattern as monotherapy; CD8/NK ratios remained favorable. No separate numeric table for combo vs mono.
title: Nemvaleukin alfa, a modified interleukin-2 cytokine, as monotherapy and with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced solid tumors (ARTISTRY-1)

baseline_value: C1D1 baseline; per-patient only
post_value: Treg fold change ~2x (FCBmax at C1D5); CD8+/NK expansion larger at C1D8/C2D22
change_magnitude: Tregs ~2x FCBmax; effector cells (CD8+/NK) FCB larger at D8/D22; post-ICI melanoma and RCC both show this pattern
clinical_correlate: Modest activity post-ICI
notes: Calvo 2025 ARTISTRY-1 Part B post-ICI cohort. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: consistent ~2x Treg FCBmax limited to C1D5, contrasting with sustained effector expansion at D8/D22.
title: Nemvaleukin alfa monotherapy in patients with advanced melanoma and renal cell carcinoma: results from the phase 1/2 non-randomized ARTISTRY-1 trial

baseline_value: C1D1 baseline; per-patient only
post_value: Low-level transient non-dose-dependent Treg expansion; favorable effector:Treg ratios maintained
change_magnitude: Minimal Treg expansion across schedules; figures only
clinical_correlate: Dose-schedule optimization
notes: Piha-Paul 2025 ARTISTRY-3 less-frequent dosing. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: 'low-level, transient, non-dose-dependent expansion of Tregs' with favorable NK:Treg and CD8:Treg ratios; Treg quantitative data in Fig 6 only.
title: Less frequent intravenous dosing of nemvaleukin alfa in patients with advanced solid tumors: the phase 1/2 ARTISTRY-3 trial

baseline_value: Most patients had <1% FoxP3 at screening; 4 patients had >1% FoxP3 at baseline
post_value: 3 of 4 high-baseline patients had on-treatment FoxP3 decrease; non-dose-dependent
change_magnitude: Inconsistent decreases limited to the 4/53 patients with detectable baseline Tregs; 3/4 of those decreased; non-dose-dependent across 10-1500 mg range
notes: Piha-Paul 2021 JITC GWN323 phase I. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: authors explicitly note 'because most patients have no Foxp3 Treg cells in the tumor microenvironment at baseline, it may be impossible to test the hypothesis that GWN323 decreases Treg cells'; only 4 patients had assessable baseline Tregs; 3/4 decreased; non-dose-dependent. Classic anti-GITR human mixed result; underpowered for depletion claim.
title: First-in-human phase I/Ib open-label dose-escalation study of GWN323 (anti-GITR) as a single agent and in combination with spartalizumab (anti-PD-1) in patients with advanced solid tumors and lymphomas

intervention: TRX518 (anti-GITR) single agent or combo (gemcitabine / pembrolizumab / nivolumab)
baseline_value: Baseline GITR+ Treg frequency: mean 41.82%, range 9.11-100% (Part C gem+TRX518 cohort); mean 36.63%, range 12.25-54.95% (Parts D+E anti-PD-1 combo cohort)
post_value: Monotherapy (Parts A+B, n=43): SD patients (n=11) had 'more substantial decreases in peripheral Tregs and eTregs' than PD patients; GITR+ Tregs consistently reduced in all patients, greater reduction in PD; gem combo: gem reduced Tregs days 1/8, TRX518 expanded on day 2 (cycle-level oscillation); anti-PD-1 combo: less consistent, CR patient had Treg INCREASE
change_magnitude: Modest, inconsistent peripheral Treg reductions; kinetics varied by combo regimen; numeric fold-changes not text-quantified (figure-only)
change_significance: p<0.05 indicated by asterisks in Fig 3A (unpaired t-test); exact values not stated
durability_described: Combo-regimen-dependent: gem oscillation d1/8 (down) vs d2 (up with TRX518); anti-PD-1 less consistent
clinical_correlate: Limited single-agent activity
notes: Davar 2022 TRX518 Phase Ib. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: modest inconsistent peripheral Treg reductions with significant combo-regimen dependence; baseline GITR+ frequency means provided (41.82% gem cohort, 36.63% anti-PD-1 cohort); ORR 3.2-12.5% across parts. Quantitative values mostly in figures without text numbers.
title: Phase IB Study of GITR Agonist Antibody TRX518 Singly and in Combination with Gemcitabine, Pembrolizumab, or Nivolumab in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors

baseline_value: Per-tumor baseline; Fig 4A shows trajectories without tabulated density
post_value: Monotherapy (Parts A+B, n=13 evaluable): 7/13 had decreased intratumoral Tregs at C1D21; anti-PD-1 combo (Parts D+E, n=9 paired): 3 responders had 'profound intratumoral Treg reductions', 6 non-responders had increased or stable tumor Tregs; gem combo (Part C, n=21): no consistent change, clinical-benefit patients had INCREASED GITR+ Treg infiltration
change_magnitude: Monotherapy: 7/13 (54%) decreased tumor Tregs; anti-PD-1 combo: 3/3 responders deep-depleters vs 6/6 non-responders not; gem combo: paradoxical increase in clinical benefiters
change_significance: Paired t-test performed on Fig 4B; exact p not stated in text
clinical_correlate: Anti-PD-1 combo responders had profound tumor Treg reductions + CD8+ TIL increases + granzyme B+ CD8+ expansion
notes: Davar 2022 TRX518 tumor. Full-text backfill 2026-04-23: 7/13 monotherapy patients depleted; 3/3 anti-PD-1 responders vs 0/6 non-responders (striking correlation); gem combo paradoxically enriched GITR+ Tregs in responders.
title: Phase IB Study of GITR Agonist Antibody TRX518 Singly and in Combination with Gemcitabine, Pembrolizumab, or Nivolumab in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors
